flashcards.back: The Z-Buffer algorithm is a depth-buffer method that resolves visibility by storing the depth of the nearest polygon at each pixel during rendering, ensuring that the frontmost surface is displayed.

flashcards.front: How are depth values stored and compared in Z-Buffer?
flashcards.back: Each pixel has a depth buffer (Z-Buffer) that stores the depth of the current nearest polygon; new polygons compute their depth at that pixel and replace the buffer value only if their depth is smaller (closer to the viewer).

flashcards.front: What is the time and space complexity of the Z-Buffer algorithm?
flashcards.back: Time complexity is O(P × R) where P is the number of polygons and R is the resolution (pixels), and space complexity is O(R) for the depth buffer (width × height).

flashcards.front: What is the List Priority (Painter's) algorithm?
flashcards.back: The List Priority algorithm (also called Painter's algorithm) sorts all polygons by their depth (typically the farthest first) and draws them in that order, so nearer polygons overwrite farther ones.

flashcards.front: Compare Z-Buffer and Painter's algorithm in terms of handling intersecting polygons.
flashcards.back: Z-Buffer can correctly render intersecting polygons because it resolves visibility per pixel, whereas Painter's algorithm fails or requires splitting polygons to handle intersections, often producing visual artifacts.

flashcards.front: What role does perspective division play in depth buffering?
flashcards.back: Perspective division converts view-space depth (z) to normalized device coordinates (NDC), making the depth value linear in screen space and enabling accurate comparison in the Z-Buffer.

flashcards.front: Provide a concise outline of the Z-Buffer algorithm steps.
flashcards.back: Initialize depth buffer to maximum depth; for each polygon, rasterize it and for each pixel compute depth; if computed depth < stored depth, update depth buffer and draw the pixel.

flashcards.front: In what scenarios is Z-Buffer preferred over List Priority algorithms?
flashcards.back: Z-Buffer is preferred when scenes contain many complex, overlapping, or intersecting polygons, or when hardware acceleration is available, despite higher memory usage.
